_comment: Data-quality flags for the human-40 eval set (user review 2026-07-13). Excluded ids are absent from human40_labels.json so eval_human_labels.py skips them automatically.

excluded.t15: not a usable tongue image (bad framing/quality)
excluded.t22: two tongues in frame — segmentation & analysis undefined

quality_flags.t24: tongue is rotated — kept, but a good stress case for orientation-robust zoning

partial_labels.t05: zhi (body colour) left unlabeled — coating too greasy to judge body colour; scored as N/A

proposed_new_label_fields._comment: Fields to add in the next labeling round (user offered yes/no or scale). These become the ground truth for the new measured features.
proposed_new_label_fields.red_tip: none | mild | strong — is the tip visibly redder than the rest of the body?
proposed_new_label_fields.red_dots: none | few | many — red dots / prickles (raised red papillae) on the surface
proposed_new_label_fields.surface_pattern: none | present — a papillae texture/pattern strong enough that greasiness can't be judged from it
proposed_new_label_fields.coating_obscures_body: no | yes — coating is so thick/pale in the centre that body colour is unreadable there (see t05)
proposed_new_label_fields.tip_shape_ambiguous: no | yes — tip is an odd shape that could be a tooth-mark or just anatomy
proposed_new_label_fields.moisture: dry | normal | wet — how wet/glossy the tongue surface looks (to validate the new gloss-based 'wet' detector; note: 'dry' can't be inferred from photos alone, so a human 'dry' label is especially valuable)
